title: Research of the Usage of Creative Drama Method in 7th Grade English Teaching
author: Çakıroğlu, Akgül
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: The aim of this research is to describe the effect of teaching English lessons with Creative Drama Method on students' attitudes and opinions. In the research, “Do students' attitudes and views towards English lesson change with the creative drama method?” answer was sought. The study group of the research, in which mixed research design was used, consists of 8 students attending the 7th grade in a primary school in Akhisar. The English course "Sports" and "Biography" units were taught in a 6-week period with the prepared creative drama method learning draft. A mixed model consisting of quantitative and qualitative research methods was used in the research. Quantitative data were obtained from the student attitude scale and qualitative data were obtained from student opinions. In this research, quantitative data were analyzed with non-parametrik mann whitney u for related samples. In the analysis of qualitative data, inductive analysis was used. In the research, as a result of the attitude scale, creative drama activities developed positive attitudes of the students towards the lesson, and as a result of the students' opinions, the drama method made the lesson fun and the learning environment became more productive, increased the motivation towards the lesson, and developed a positive attitude; In addition, the method is effective in learning vocabulary and pronunciation in a foreign language; Finally, it was concluded that the students' creative drama method can be effective in other lessons, especially in foreign language lessons.
